_source.title: Monitoring the quality of fresh-cut Kimju guava in packages using intelligent chitosan film incorporated with AMP and anthocyanin
_source.abstract: This research was conducted to study the use of 0.5 percent chitosan film mixed with 10 percent aminomethyl propanol (AMP) and 1 percent anthocyanin from butterfly pea flowers that coated on filter papers for monitoring the quality of fresh-cut Kimju guava stored at 10 and 37 deg C. The results found that Kimju guava packages had increases of CO2 concentration and decreases of O2 concentration. Fresh-cut guava showed increases of pH, total soluble solid, and total bacterial counts. Moreover, the browning and spoilage of guava flesh increased as the storage temperature increased. On the 7th day of storage at 10 and 37 deg C, CO2 concentrations in the packages were increased from 0 to 0.5 and 5 percent. The pH values were increased from 4.2 to 4.5 and 5.9. Total soluble solids increased from 24.6 to 28.4 and 28.2 Brix. Total bacteria, yeasts, and molds were increased from 5.6, 4.8, and 2.5 log CFU/g to 17, 8.7, and 15 log CFU/g and 20.5, 9.7, and 19.5 log CFU/g, respectively. Escherichia coli and Salmonella spp. were not detected in fresh-cut guava stored at 10 deg C, but E. coli (8.1 log CFU/g) was detected when stored at 37 deg C. The chitosan film color was changed from blue to blue-green and blue-yellow according to the loss of guava quality during storage. Therefore, the intelligent chitosan film could be used as an index indicator for monitoring the quality of fresh-cut guava.

_source.title: Extraction of chitosan from mussel shells for application in biodegradable film production
_source.abstract: In this research study, a facile chemical extraction method to obtain chitosan from mussel shells was employed, achieving high yields of the product. Subsequently, we proceeded to fabricate films using the extracted chitosan. The results demonstrated the successful production of good-quality films. These films exhibited translucency and displayed improved properties compared to films produced without the incorporation of chitosan.

_source.title: Effects of chitosan coating on prolonging of shelf life, egg quality and antimicrobial of chicken eggs
_source.abstract: The objective of this study was to evaluate the efficiency of chitosan coating on prolonging shelf life, egg quality and antimicrobial of chicken eggs. The experiment was randomly allotted into a completely randomized design. Chitosan coatings eggs were divided into 5 treatments according to the concentration of chitosan coated eggs with 0, 0.5, 1, 1.5 and 2%, respectively. Chicken eggs were storage at 25 deg C and 50% relative humidity for 33 days. The results showed that haugh unit (HU) of eggs decreased when the storage period increased. The quality of uncoated egg decreased from haugh unit = 72.06 to 68.62 after 6 days of storage. While, all chitosan coated eggs could be maintain the quality of eggs (haugh unit 72) in AA grade for 9 days. However, egg weight, egg yolk color, weight loss and antimicrobial were no significant difference (P more than 0.05) between treatments. After 33 days of storage, non-coated eggs had 8.89% of weight loss higher than the weight loss of 1 and 1.5 % of chitosan coated eggs 7.86 and 8.23%. The quality of chicken egg coated with chitosan had significantly better quality than non-coated eggs and room temperature storage at 25 deg C for at least 33 days. In conclusion, the application showed of chitosan coated eggs the effectively extended the shelf life of fresh eggs at 0, 0.5, 1, 1.5 and 2 % of chitosan concentration. The quality of chitosan coated eggs in haugh unit 72 can be preserved for up to 9 days at 25 deg C.

_source.title: Effect of vitamin c and chitosan film coating on storability of sunflower seeds
_source.abstract: Sunflower seeds have a high fat content that causes rapid seed deterioration. The objective of this experiment was to determine the effects of coating of sunflower seeds with polymer (Flare-2, Centor Thai) mixing with vitamin c and chitosan at the concentrations of 0 (control) 2 4 and 6 ppm (120 ml/100g seed). The coated seeds were stored in plastic bag for 6 months at 10 deg C. The results showed that the seed germination of coated seeds for all treatments after 4 months of storage was not significantly different from the initial value. However, after 5 and 6 months of storage, the control treatment had the lowest germination percentage of 76 percent and it was lower than the coated seeds with polymer mixing with vitamin C and chitosan treatments (87-95 percent). Seed vigor of coated seeds decreased with the longer storage time in every treatment. Germination index (GI) and seedling growth

_source.title: Effect of chitosan on yield and quality of cucumber (Cucumis sativus l.)
_source.abstract: The study on effects of chitosan on yield and quality of cucumber were investigated. The experiment was used with a Completely Randomized Design (CRD) with 20 replications and 7 treatments as by chosen concentration levels of 0, 0.5, 1, 1.5, 2, 2.5 and 3 ml/L. Chitosan was applied every 2 weeks. The number of fruit per plant, fruit weight, fruit length, diameter, firmness and sweetness of the cucumber were recorded. The results showed that the T7 chitosan at 3 ml/L significantly increased the highest yield of 15 fruits per plant. However, while the T7 had a significantly lower sweetness of the cucumber than the T4, T5 and T6 chitosan applied at 1.5, 2 and 2.5 ml/L respectively. In addition, the application of chitosan at 0.5 and 1 ml/L both T2 and T3 was significantly higher fruit weight per fruit of the cucumber when compared to T7 treatment

_source.title: Synthesis of silver particles using white sugar for shelf-life extension of banana and tomato
_source.abstract: Silver particle was prepared with green method using white sugar as a reducing agent and coated on paper packaging for shelf-life extension of vegetable and fruit.The disinfection efficiency against E. coli was investigated with initial concentration of E. coli bacteria of 2*10**(3) CFU/ml.The result showed that paper coated with chitosan and silver nitrates synthesized by white sugar inhibit 100 percent of E. coli for 10 minutes. In addition, shelf-life extension of banana and tomato using paper coated with chitosan and silver nitrates synthesized by white sugar at room temperature (25 deg C) for 7 and 14 days, respectively. The result found that the shelf-life extension of banana and tomato could be increased by packing with paper packing coated with chitosan and silver nitrates synthesized by white sugar.

_source.title: Effects of chitosan on growth and level of leaves brown spot disease in Hom-nin rice
_source.abstract: Chitosan foliar application on growth and level of leaves brown spot disease incidencein Homnin rice.The experiment was 2x6 factorial in CRD5 replications including 2 factors. Factor 1) concentrations of chitosan with 1.2 and 2.4 percent, factor 2) volumes of chitosan at 0, 2, 4, 8, 10 and 16 ml/l. The results showed that chitosan improved rice growth and positively related with chlorophyll content. The application of chitosan at 2.4 percent increased chlorophyll content higher than that at 1.2 percent. The application of chitosan at 10 ml/l by volume provided maximum chlorophyll content. The increase in the volume of chitosan resulted in lower chlorophyll content. Furthermore, chitosan application reduced severity of leaves brown spot disease and disease index in Hom-nin rice. The application of chitosan at 1.2 percent and 2.4 percent with 10 ml/L by volume showed the lowerse verity of brown spot disease in leaves, related to increasing salicylic acid content in leaves. Thus, this alleviated leaves brown spot disease symptoms in Hom-nin rice
